what_it_does: Declares a runtime-checkable Protocol named Resolver that defines the contract for a storage backend supporting immutable content (cas://), mutable pointers (run://), provenance/lineage, and a memoization gate.
observations: All methods are abstract stubs ending in '...'
Methods are grouped by comments: immutable content, mutable pointer, provenance + lineage, memo
Type hints use str, Any, None, list[str], and Provenance
Docstring references 'C4 — the Resolver Protocol' and build-prep/contracts/C4
standouts: It bundles content-addressable storage, mutable refs, provenance/lineage, and memoization in one interface
The memo methods are explicitly labeled 'the gate'
conventions: Uses type annotations and future annotations
Groups related methods with inline section comments
Uses address-style naming: cas:// and run://
concerns: No method-level docstrings or error contract
lineage returns list[str] but ordering and depth semantics are unspecified
runtime_checkable only checks method signatures, not behavior
notes: This file is the central interface contract for a storage resolver that separates immutable content, mutable references, provenance, and memoization.
questions: What exactly is the C4 protocol referenced in the docstring?
How is lineage ordering defined: nearest-first or root-first?
What error should callers expect when a cas or run address is missing?
What is the expected format and scope of a memo sig?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it defines a protocol for resolving immutable content, mutable pointers, provenance, and memoization.
apparent_themes: Separation of immutable and mutable state
intention_signals: The file appears to be establishing a stable backend contract so that filesystem and Supabase implementations can be swapped while preserving reproducibility, traceability, and caching behavior.
novelty: It treats memoization as a first-class responsibility of the resolver rather than a separate layer, and exposes lineage traversal as part of the storage contract.
contribution: Provides a unified interface contract that a fused system can keep to ensure any storage backend supports content storage, named references, provenance, and memoized lookups consistently.
summary_for_embedding: A runtime-checkable Python Protocol defining the Resolver interface for content-addressable storage, mutable run:// references, provenance/lineage, and memoization.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to formalize the C4 Resolver contract that storage backends must implement.

symbols.description: A Protocol class annotated with @runtime_checkable that serves as the abstract contract for a storage resolver, grouping methods for immutable content, mutable references, provenance, and memoization.

symbols.does: Stores immutable content and returns its content-addressed identifier.
symbols.description: Accepts arbitrary data and returns a cas:// address string, enabling content-addressable storage where the same data always maps to the same identifier.

symbols.does: Retrieves immutable content by its cas:// address.
symbols.description: Takes a content-address string and returns the stored data object associated with it.

symbols.does: Checks whether content exists for a given cas:// address.
symbols.description: Returns a boolean indicating if the resolver currently stores data for the supplied content address.

symbols.does: Updates a mutable logical pointer to point at a cas:// address.
symbols.description: Binds a run:// logical name to a specific content address, allowing mutable references over immutable content.

symbols.does: Returns the current cas:// address of a mutable logical pointer.
symbols.description: Looks up the latest content address bound to a run:// logical name, returning None if unset.

symbols.does: Records provenance metadata for an artifact.
symbols.description: Persists a Provenance object so the system can later query how an artifact was produced.

symbols.does: Retrieves provenance metadata for an address.
symbols.description: Returns the Provenance record associated with a given address, or None if none exists.

symbols.does: Walks input dependencies back toward source addresses.
symbols.description: Returns a list of address strings representing the transitive input lineage for the supplied address, supporting traceability from outputs to sources.

symbols.does: Looks up a cached result by computation signature.
symbols.description: Takes a memoization signature and returns the previously stored content address if the computation has already been run.

symbols.does: Stores a cached mapping from computation signature to result address.
symbols.description: Associates a memoization signature with a content address so future identical computations can reuse the result.
